items.title: As the ceasefire cracks, Iran got 'a complete reset' of the pressure on its oil trade

children.content: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

articles.title: US carries out strikes on Iran after Donald Trump declares ceasefire 'over'

children.content: The United States military is carrying out renewed strikes on Iran aimed at further degrading its ability to "threaten freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz", after US President Donald Trump earlier threatened a "big attack" was coming.

image.altText: president trump's face close up - looking displease

gallery.altText: A gas flare on an oil production platform in the Soroush oil fields is seen alongside an Iranian flag in the Persian Gulf, Iran

thumbnail.altText: A gas flare on an oil production platform in the Soroush oil fields is seen alongside an Iranian flag in the Persian Gulf, Iran

items.title: 'Pretty cool': Trump announces win for Ukraine amid fiery NATO meeting

children.content: Donald Trump says his country will probably continue to attack Iran and says Ukraine will be given permission to manufacture much-needed Patriot missiles as it fights Russia's full-scale invasion.

image.altText: Two men in suits sitting and speaking.

items.title: Praise and protests as Modi arrives in Melbourne

children.content: A large swathe of the Indian diaspora wants to warmly welcome Narendra Modi to Melbourne, but others demand Australia put pressure on him over his human rights record.

image.altText: A man holds his hands together in a praying symbol as he looks at six people standing in a line.

items.title: External oversight of ABC needs overhaul, Segal tells royal commission

children.content: Australia's Envoy to Combat Antisemitism, Jillian Segal, has accused the ABC of being its own "judge, counsel and jury", recommending an independent oversight committee be established to review the public broadcaster's compliance with its charter obligations.

image.altText: Ms Segal sitting at a bench with two microphones in front of her.

items.title: Trainee surgeon pleads guilty to secretly filming hundreds of victims

children.content: Ryan Cho pleaded guilty to 13 charges including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

image.altText: Ryan Cho wears sunglasses and a surgical mask as he walks down the street. A reporter holds a microphone towards him.

items.title: Telstra solves 'software defect' after hundreds of failed Triple Zero calls

children.content: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

articles.title: Telstra confirms Triple Zero services are back online

children.content: Triple Zero services are back online following Telstra's outage. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Woman looking at mobile phone without service, SOS message

articles.title: Victoria's regional rail services resume after Telstra outage

children.content: V/Line is gradually resuming services from midday after a Telstra outage brought the state's regional train network to a standstill.

image.altText: V Line trains

articles.title: Why a telco outage has stopped some trains but not others

children.content: Trains relying on the 4G network of freight rail track operator ARTC went down amid the Telstra failure.

image.altText: a regional train station with a line closure warning sign

articles.title: 'Shouldn't occur': Change demanded after Telstra outage

children.content: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

image.altText: A close up of a Telstra logo on the side of a pay phone

gallery.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

thumbnail.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

items.title: 'Cannot remain silent': Egypt demands FIFA investigate World Cup referees

children.content: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Mohamed Salah and the Egypt team argue with a referee

items.title: FIFA boss referred to Olympics ethics board over Trump World Cup call

children.content: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino standing with each other

items.title: Neil the seal may have left Tasmania, wildlife experts say

children.content: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

items.title: Growers push to overturn ban on ancient 'superfood'

children.content: Farmers are appealing against a federal ban on the sale of
Moringa oleifera
as a food after a regulatory body determined there was insufficient evidence to prove its safety.

image.altText: A man holding up two pots in a greenhouse surrounded by small trees in pots.

items.title: Search for crew continues after Boeing cargo plane crash

children.content: Rescue crews battle rough sea conditions to recover parts of a Boeing cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea. Five crew members remain missing.

image.altText: Rescuers wearing safety gear unload debris from a small boat

items.title: 'Important' Aboriginal language statement released amid 'abysmal' loss

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

articles.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

image.altText: podcast image NAIDOC week

items.title: The key ingredient keeping the snow season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

image.altText: A group of skiers on the perisher mountainside with ski lifts in the background.

items.title: How new Blues bash brothers blasted their way to State of Origin glory

children.content: Liam Martin and Hudson Young don't like to point fingers, unless it comes time to decide which of the New South Wales backrowers is madder.

image.altText: Liam Martin runs the ball during an Origin match

items.title: Rob Reiner nominated for posthumous Emmy for The Bear

children.content: Plus Connor Storrie nabs his first nom but not for Heated Rivalry, Hacks soars on a swan song and more successes, surprises and snubs from today's Emmy nominations.

items.title: The grief of childlessness will always be with me

children.content: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

image.altText: April and Sally smiling at each other on the couch

children.content: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

children.content: Can water be made from air in WA's wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

items.title: Judge approves $8m payment to E Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse case

children.content: A Manhattan judge orders the payment to the former Elle magazine advice columnist, who a jury found was sexually abused by Donald Trump.

image.altText: A woman with a blonde bob and wearing sunglasses smiles outside court.

items.title: 'Going to make it double': Boxer's jokes about domestic violence condemned

children.content: A leading violence prevention organisation says an Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence is unacceptable.

image.altText: Australian boxer Benjamin Hussain deleted a video joking about domestic violence

items.title: Racist commentary called out in World Cup coverage

children.content: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

image.altText: Cote d'Ivoire defender Ghislain Konan runs with the ball during a game

items.title: Swimming Australia fights 'scary' fake news in world-first initiative

children.content: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: Streets reopen around New York high-rise after buckled beams stabilised

children.content: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

gallery.altText: Three people in hard hats and hi-vis vests are seen through a window, inspecting a support beam.

thumbnail.altText: Three people in hard hats and hi-vis vests are seen through a window, inspecting a support beam.

items.title: 'We'll sell his body parts': AI extortionists target missing man's parents

children.content: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

image.altText: A man with short dark hair and a close beard wearing a khaki green t-shirt sitting outside a playground.

items.title: Record fine for gold miner thrusts tiny bat into spotlight

children.content: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

image.altText: A small bat is held with two hands with its wings spread apart.

items.title: Farmers search for ways to break free from controversial herbicide

children.content: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap, crouching in a paddock of wheat.

items.title: Australians face record-high rents as agents see 'inquiries flooding in'

children.content: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

image.altText: A for lease sign at a picket fence

children.content: The case of Ruth Ellis has gripped Britain for decades and inspired the 1985 film Dance with a Stranger.

items.title: Aboriginal woman fights trespass charge, cites 'cultural obligation'

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: The UK's next PM has a carefully curated image. Not everyone is buying it

children.content: For some, Andy Burnham's casual communication style is refreshing. Others say it's a carefully crafted facade, disguising the fact that he's a ruthless Labour Party lifer.

image.altText: A man wearing a t-shirt and suit jacket, standing at a lecturn and smiling

items.title: 'Can't believe it': British wildcard books Wimbledon semifinal spot

children.content: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

articles.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run as attacks hit Kyiv

children.content: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

image.altText: Marta Kostyuk lifts both her hands in the air to celebrate, on the green grass of a Wimbledon court.

articles.title: Aussie doubles fly the flag at Wimbledon as mixed pair makes final

children.content: Marc Polmans and Storm Hunter win through to the Wimbledon mixed doubles final, as Thanasi Kokkinakis flies the flag in the men's doubles.

image.altText: A tennis player plays a shot close to the net.

gallery.altText: Arthur Fery doffs his cap to the crowd after his win

items.title: Can Fremantle keep rolling to the AFL minor premiership?

children.content: Fremantle has lost its 14-game winning streak with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

image.altText: Four Fremantle AFL players (including three talls) stand in the rooms singing the club song after a win.

items.title: Emmy nominations 2026 revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list

children.content: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

image.altText: THE PITT

items.title: Jake answers the call and saddles up for ride of a lifetime

children.content: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.
